title: Daily reconcile job at 02:00 Europe/Rome is silently skipped on DST spring-forward
preconditions: Today is the last Sunday of March in the Europe/Rome timezone (DST spring-forward).
The reconcile job is scheduled at 02:00 Europe/Rome via APScheduler CronTrigger.
steps: (scheduler) Wait for 01:59 Europe/Rome on the spring-forward Sunday; observe the clock jump directly to 03:00
(observer) Inspect the scheduler log around 02:00–03:00 and report whether the reconcile.daily fire-event is present
(observer) Inspect finance@ inbox and report whether the daily summary email arrived
(observer) Inspect the reconciled-rows table for that date and report the row count
expected: The reconcile job runs exactly once on the spring-forward day (either at the start of the new local time or with an explicit DST-shift warning logged).
actual: APScheduler skips the 02:00 fire because that hour does not exist; no log entry; no summary email; the reconciled-rows table for that date has zero entries until manual rerun.
suggested_fix_direction: Schedule the cron in UTC and document the corresponding local-time semantics. If a local-time requirement is firm, switch to APScheduler's IntervalTrigger or to a timezone-aware library that emits an explicit warning and shifts to the next valid local time.
